Point Url and Homepage to castrojo.github.io/testhub and
github.com/castrojo/testhub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add io.github.kolunmi.Saturn

Saturn is a GNOME GTK4/libadwaita Flatpak launcher and application
manager by Eva M (kolunmi). Built with Meson (C) and a Common Lisp
backend via ECL (Embeddable Common Lisp).

Build notes:
- ECL compiled from source (tag 24.5.10) — first build is slow
- 15 CL library dependencies, all pinned to specific commits
- Runtime: org.gnome.Platform//50
- x-skip-install-test: true for bootstrap (remove after first index commit)

Broad filesystem permissions (--filesystem=home, /var/lib/flatpak,
--talk-name=org.freedesktop.Flatpak) are justified for a Flatpak
manager application.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add finish-args-flatpak-system-folder-access lint exception

Saturn needs /var/lib/flatpak access (system-wide flatpak install dir)
since it's a Flatpak launcher/manager.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): rename app-id → id for flatpak-builder-lint exception resolution

flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads the 'id' key, not
'app-id'. When appid cannot be inferred, exceptions are never applied
and all errors are reported unconditionally. Renaming to 'id' (the
canonical form accepted by both flatpak-builder and the linter) allows
our user-exceptions to take effect.

Root cause: flatpak_builder_lint/manifest.py infer_appid returns None
for manifests using the legacy 'app-id' key.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add appstream-missing-screenshots builddir lint exception

manifest-lint uses 'metainfo-missing-screenshots', builddir-lint uses
the distinct key 'appstream-missing-screenshots'. Both needed for Saturn
since we have no upstream screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add repo-stage screenshot lint exception

Three lint stages use three distinct exception keys for screenshots:
- manifest:  metainfo-missing-screenshots
- builddir:  appstream-missing-screenshots
- repo:      appstream-screenshots-not-mirrored-in-ostree

All three needed since Saturn has no screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): use app-id key instead of id

testhub's Justfile metadata recipe looks for 'app-id' key;
flatpak-builder accepts both 'id' and 'app-id' but testhub
convention (all other manifests) is 'app-id'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(justfile): support 'id:' as alias for 'app-id:' in manifest lookup

Newer Flatpak manifests use 'id:' as the canonical field (flatpak-builder
accepts both; flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads 'id:').
Saturn uses 'id:' to allow user-exceptions to be resolved by app ID.

Updated all Justfile lookups to handle both keys:
- metadata recipe: falls back to '.id' when '.app-id' is empty
- _compile/_repack/run-test: yq '.".app-id" // .id' for direct reads
- grep-based directory lookups: '-lE (app-id|id): $APP'

All existing apps using 'app-id:' continue to work unchanged.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(saturn): remove x-skip-install-test bootstrap flag

Build 5 (run 23395028554) succeeded — Saturn is now indexed in the
testhub gh-pages repo. Real e2e install test can now run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(skills): capture Saturn onboarding lessons

- Add saturn to app-gotchas.md (ECL time, three-stage lint exceptions,
  app-id key requirement, x-skip-launch-check, bootstrap pattern)
- Add two-build bootstrap pattern to pipeline.md
- Add three lint stages section to pipeline.md
- Add manifest key convention (app-id: vs id:) to pipeline.md

Lessons from Saturn CI run 23395028554 (feat/saturn branch).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add x-skip-launch-check for headless CI

Saturn is a GTK4 GUI app — flatpak run exits 1 with 'Failed to open
display' in the headless gnome-50 container. x-skip-launch-check skips
the launch test while preserving the flatpak install verification.

Closes castrojo/testhub#6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pr61): address Copilot review comments

- grep -lE -> grep -lF (4 locations): app IDs contain dots which are
  regex wildcards in ERE mode; -F fixed-string avoids accidental matches
- testhub.flatpakrepo: revert fork-specific castrojo URLs to projectbluefin
- build.yml: remove non-existent e2e-install from container comment
- docs: update app-id vs id guidance in pipeline.md, app-gotchas.md,
  and flatpaks/saturn/GOTCHAS.md to reflect Justfile now supporting both keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Claude/opencode hooks plugin support (#31)

* feat: add OpenCode plugin support

Adds a native TypeScript plugin (plugin/opencode.ts) that integrates
otel-hook with OpenCode's plugin system, following the same thin-wrapper
pattern as rtk.

- plugin/opencode.ts: exports OtelHookPlugin; checks otel-hook is in
  PATH at load time, then hooks session.created/deleted → SessionStart/
  SessionEnd and tool.execute.before/after → PreToolUse/PostToolUse,
  piping JSON payloads to otel-hook via stdin with IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode

- setup.sh: adds --opencode flag and setup_opencode() function that
  copies the plugin to .opencode/plugins/ (project) or
  ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ (--global); auto-detects OpenCode via
  command -v opencode or ~/.config/opencode; respects OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR

- examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts: annotated copy for users who
  want to customise the plugin

- README.md: replaces the placeholder OpenCode blurb with a proper
  setup section including quick-start commands and manual install steps

- tests/test_otel_hook.py: adds TestOpenCodePluginPayloads (11 tests)
  covering IDE detection, event normalisation, and priority ordering for
  all four payload types emitted by the plugin

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): handle all observable hook events

Extends plugin/opencode.ts (and the matching example) to cover the full
set of OpenCode events that map to canonical otel-hook events:

  session.idle    → Stop            (agent finished responding)
  message.updated → UserPromptSubmit (role=user messages only)
  session.error   → SessionEnd with status=error (closes trace on failure)

The previous commit only wired session.created/deleted and the tool
execute lifecycle. The three new branches follow the same thin-wrapper
pattern: build a minimal JSON payload and pipe it to otel-hook via
IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode.

Tests: adds 8 new assertions to TestOpenCodePluginPayloads covering
IDE detection and event-name extraction for every payload shape the
updated plugin emits. All 185 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* docs: update Supported Events table with OpenCode plugin column

- Splits "Claude Code / Antigravity / compatible runners" into two
  separate columns: one for Claude Code / Antigravity, one for
  OpenCode (plugin) showing the native OpenCode event names that
  each canonical event maps from (session.created, session.idle,
  message.updated, tool.execute.before/after, etc.)
- Updates the "Events captured" blurb under the OpenCode setup
  section to reflect the full list (UserPromptSubmit and Stop were
  missing) and adds a note about the upstream MCP tool call limitation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(setup): add --cursor --global support

setup.sh --cursor always wrote to the project-level .cursor/hooks.json;
there was no way to target the global ~/.cursor/hooks.json without
manually editing the file.

Changes:
- Add CURSOR_GLOBAL variable; --global now sets it alongside
  CLAUDE_GLOBAL and OPENCODE_GLOBAL
- setup_cursor() branches on CURSOR_GLOBAL: global path is
  ~/.cursor/hooks.json, project path is unchanged
- Echo label updated to show (global: ...) or (project: ...) like
  the Claude and OpenCode functions already do
- Next-steps output notes when global hooks.json was written
- Usage comment and README Quick Start updated with --cursor --global

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): add AfterFileEdit and PostToolUseFailure; fix table

Source-verified against sst/opencode (dev branch) to close the gaps
in the event coverage:

plugin/opencode.ts + examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts:
- file.edited → AfterFileEdit: properties contain exactly {file:string}
  (no session_id); emitted by write/edit/apply_patch tools
- tool.execute.after now inspects output.metadata.exit (the only
  failure signal for bash); non-zero exit emits PostToolUseFailure
  with exit_code and error="exit <n>", zero/absent emits PostToolUse

README Supported Events table:
- PostToolUseFailure: tool.execute.after (exit≠0)
- AfterFileEdit: file.edited
- Added footnotes explaining that bash/read/write/MCP/subagent tool
  calls all route through tool.execute.before/after (→ PreToolUse/
  PostToolUse with the relevant tool_name)
- Removed incorrect MCP limitation note — sst/opencode wraps MCP
  calls in the same tool.execute.before/after hooks
- Updated "Events captured" blurb to list all 8 emitted event types

tests/test_otel_hook.py: 6 new assertions (25 total in
TestOpenCodePluginPayloads) covering AfterFileEdit and
PostToolUseFailure IDE detection, canonical-name check, and
_get_event_name round-trip.

commit.message:
fix: add setsid launcher to fully detach server from terminal (prevents SIGTTOU)

nohup alone doesn't prevent SIGTTOU on macOS. start_server.py calls os.setsid()
before exec-ing uvicorn, creating a new session with no controlling terminal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: manim SIGTTOU crash — use subprocess.run in executor instead of asyncio.create_subprocess_exec

Same fix as ffmpeg: nohup servers get SIGTTOU when spawning subprocesses that
probe the terminal (manim uses rich). Run in executor with close_fds=True and
stdin=DEVNULL to fully detach from terminal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: add webhook secret verification for Fal.ai webhook

Add x-fal-webhook-secret header check to prevent spoofed webhook
requests. Falls back gracefully when FAL_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set
(development mode).

Also add FAL_WEBHOOK_SECRET to .env.example.

feat: scaffold complete VideoForge AI monorepo

Sets up the full Turborepo monorepo structure for the VideoForge AI
video generation platform.

## Structure
- apps/web — Next.js 14 App Router with all pages and API routes
- apps/mobile — Expo SDK 52 React Native with tab navigation
- packages/ui — Shared shadcn/ui-based dark theme components
- packages/shared — TypeScript types, Zod schemas, pricing utilities
- packages/config — Shared tsconfig, tailwind, eslint configs

## Web app (apps/web)
Pages: landing, auth (login/register), dashboard, generate, gallery,
pricing, settings

API routes:
- /api/trpc/[trpc] — tRPC handler (generation, user, billing, character)
- /api/webhooks/fal — Fal.ai generation status webhooks
- /api/webhooks/stripe — Stripe subscription & payment webhooks

Library:
- Firebase Auth + Firestore (client & admin)
- Fal.ai client for AI video generation
- BullMQ + Redis job queue with tier-based priority
- Cloudflare R2 for permanent video storage
- Stripe for subscriptions and credit purchases

## Mobile app (apps/mobile)
- Generate screen with prompt input, duration slider, aspect ratio
- Gallery screen with infinite scroll video grid
- Profile screen with stats and plan info
- Login/register screen with Firebase Auth

## Pricing & model routing
- Free: fal-ai/longcat 480p $0.005/s (3 videos/day)
- Creator: fal-ai/wan v2.2 720p $0.025/video (50/month, $19/mo)
- Pro: fal-ai/kling v2.6 1080p $0.07-0.14/s (200/month, $49/mo)
- Studio: fal-ai/kling v3 1080p $0.224-0.336/s (unlimited, $149/mo)
- 1 credit = $0.10; costs rounded up to nearest credit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
